image_description: A diagram showing the structure of the blood-brain barrier, highlighting its selective permeability.
learning_objective: Understand the role and function of the blood-brain barrier in protecting the central nervous system.
tested_fact_block_id: INFERRED: The blood-brain barrier (BBB) is a semipermeable barrier that protects the central nervous system from potentially harmful substances.

image_description: A diagram showing acetylcholinesterase breaking down acetylcholine in the synaptic cleft.
learning_objective: Understand the specific enzyme responsible for the inactivation of acetylcholine and its location of action.

image_description: A diagram showing the electron transfer process during oxidation, highlighting the disruption of cellular components.
learning_objective: Understand the role of oxidation in cellular damage and its implications in behavioral neuroscience.

image_description: A diagram showing a neurotransmitter binding to a receptor on the postsynaptic membrane.
learning_objective: Understand the role of receptors in neurotransmission.

image_description: A diagram showing the glutamate-glutamine cycle between astrocytes and neurons.
learning_objective: Understand the role of astrocytes in neurotransmitter metabolism and recycling.

image_description: A diagram showing the enzymatic synthesis of GABA from glutamate.
learning_objective: Identify the enzyme responsible for synthesizing GABA from glutamate.

image_description: A diagram showing the presynaptic terminal with calcium channels and synaptic vesicles.
learning_objective: Understand the role of calcium ions in triggering neurotransmitter release at the presynaptic terminal.

image_description: A diagram showing the role of calcium ions in synaptic vesicle fusion with the presynaptic membrane.
learning_objective: Understand the specific role of calcium ions in neurotransmitter release.

image_description: A diagram showing synaptic vesicles releasing neurotransmitters into the synaptic cleft.
learning_objective: Understand the role and function of synaptic vesicles in neurotransmission.
tested_fact_block_id: Synaptic vesicles are small, membrane-bound structures that store and release neurotransmitters.

image_description: A diagram showing the opening of voltage-dependent calcium channels in response to membrane depolarization.
learning_objective: Understand the role of voltage-dependent calcium channels in initiating neurotransmitter release.
